_comment: Mnemosyne format sustainability registry. Tiers are ADVISORY and describe FORMAT longevity by two criteria only: (1) is the format publicly documented, and (2) are there multiple independent, healthy open-source readers? Vendor financial health is NOT a criterion. Tiers: OPEN, DOCUMENTED-PROPRIETARY, AT-RISK, UNKNOWN. The optional 'role' tags a file's PURPOSE in a creative workflow — a DISCIPLINE-NEUTRAL, orthogonal dimension the dock snapshot classifies by: ORIGINALS (irreplaceable masters — camera RAWs, audio stems/multitracks, camera video originals, layered image masters), DELIVERABLES (rendered/exported outputs), SIDECARS (per-asset metadata files), PROJECT-FILES (application project/catalog files); 'critical' marks PROJECT-FILES state (e.g. .lrcat, .als, .prproj) whose loss is unrecoverable. This file is embedded in the binary; drop a formats.json in the data dir to override or extend it (entries are merged by extension, yours win).

.dng.rationale: Adobe DNG is publicly documented and TIFF/EP-based; read by many independent tools.

.tif.rationale: TIFF is an open, decades-stable spec with ubiquitous readers; a layered/16-bit TIFF is a working master, not a final deliverable.

.tiff.rationale: TIFF is an open, decades-stable spec with ubiquitous readers; a layered/16-bit TIFF is a working master.

.jpg.rationale: JPEG (ISO/IEC 10918) is a universal standard with many independent readers.

.jpeg.rationale: JPEG (ISO/IEC 10918) is a universal standard with many independent readers.

.png.rationale: PNG (ISO/IEC 15948, W3C) is an open spec with universal readers.

.gif.rationale: GIF spec is public and the LZW patent long expired; universal readers.

.bmp.rationale: BMP is a trivially documented bitmap format.

.webp.rationale: WebP has a public spec and a reference library (libwebp).

.svg.rationale: SVG is an open W3C XML vector format.

.pdf.rationale: PDF (ISO 32000) is an open spec with multiple independent readers.

.txt.rationale: Plain text has no format lock-in.

.md.rationale: Markdown is plain text with a simple, public convention.

.json.rationale: JSON is an open, plain-text data format.

.xml.rationale: XML is an open, plain-text markup standard.

.html.rationale: HTML is an open web standard.

.xmp.rationale: XMP is Adobe's publicly documented RDF/XML sidecar metadata.

.cue.rationale: A CUE sheet is a plain-text track/index map for an audio image — a sidecar, not the audio.

.srt.rationale: SubRip subtitles are plain text with a trivial, universal convention.

.vtt.rationale: WebVTT is a plain-text W3C subtitle/caption format.

.zip.rationale: ZIP has a public spec and universal readers.

.wav.rationale: WAV/RIFF PCM is open and trivially parseable — the standard container for uncompressed multitrack stems and captures.

.aif.rationale: AIFF is an open, documented PCM audio container — an uncompressed capture/stem master.

.aiff.rationale: AIFF is an open, documented PCM audio container — an uncompressed capture/stem master.

.flac.rationale: FLAC is an open spec with a reference decoder — a lossless mastered/render deliverable.

.mp3.rationale: MP3 is ubiquitous with many independent decoders (core patents expired) — a mastered render deliverable.

.ogg.rationale: Ogg Vorbis is an open, documented lossy audio format with a reference codec.

.opus.rationale: Opus (RFC 6716) is an open, royalty-free audio codec with a reference implementation.

.mp4.rationale: MP4 (ISO base media file format) is an ISO standard read by many tools — the common delivery container.

.m4v.rationale: M4V is an MP4 (ISO base media file format) variant read by many tools.

.avi.rationale: AVI/RIFF is an old, documented container read everywhere.

.mkv.rationale: Matroska is an open container spec with a reference library.

.nef.rationale: Nikon raw: the spec is closed, but libraw and dcraw decode it robustly.
.nef.migration: Retain the original NEF; consider archiving a DNG sibling for extra longevity.

.cr2.rationale: Canon raw (CR2): closed spec, decoded robustly by libraw/dcraw.
.cr2.migration: Retain the original CR2; consider archiving a DNG sibling.

.cr3.rationale: Canon raw (CR3): closed spec, decoded by recent libraw and exiftool.
.cr3.migration: Retain the original CR3; consider archiving a DNG sibling.

.arw.rationale: Sony raw: closed spec, decoded by libraw/dcraw.
.arw.migration: Retain the original ARW; consider archiving a DNG sibling.

.raf.rationale: Fujifilm raw: closed spec, decoded by libraw.
.raf.migration: Retain the original RAF; consider archiving a DNG sibling.

.orf.rationale: Olympus raw: closed spec, decoded by libraw.
.orf.migration: Retain the original ORF; consider archiving a DNG sibling.

.rw2.rationale: Panasonic raw: closed spec, decoded by libraw.
.rw2.migration: Retain the original RW2; consider archiving a DNG sibling.

.pef.rationale: Pentax raw: closed spec, decoded by libraw.
.pef.migration: Retain the original PEF; consider archiving a DNG sibling.

.3fr.rationale: Hasselblad raw: closed spec, decoded by libraw.
.3fr.migration: Retain the original; consider archiving a DNG sibling.

.psd.rationale: Photoshop document: closed spec, read by GIMP, ImageMagick, and psd_tools — a layered working master, not a final deliverable.
.psd.migration: For long-term access, also export a flattened TIFF or PNG alongside the PSD.

.psb.rationale: Large Photoshop document: closed spec, partial open readers; a layered working master.
.psb.migration: Export a flattened TIFF alongside the PSB.

.heic.rationale: HEIF/HEVC (ISO/IEC 23008-12) is documented but HEVC is patent-encumbered; libheif decodes it.
.heic.migration: Consider archiving a JPEG or TIFF sibling for unencumbered access.

.heif.rationale: HEIF (ISO/IEC 23008-12) is documented but codec-encumbered; libheif decodes it.
.heif.migration: Consider archiving a JPEG or TIFF sibling.

.m4a.rationale: MPEG-4 audio (AAC/ALAC in an MP4 container): documented but codec-encumbered; ffmpeg decodes it.
.m4a.migration: For an unencumbered master, keep the source WAV/AIFF stems; consider a FLAC sibling.

.aac.rationale: AAC is documented (ISO/IEC 14496-3) but patent-encumbered; ffmpeg decodes it.
.aac.migration: Keep the source stems; consider a FLAC sibling for an unencumbered render.

.mov.rationale: QuickTime container: documented and widely read, but codecs may be encumbered — commonly a delivery rendition.
.mov.migration: For long-term access, consider a widely-supported MP4/H.264 rendition.

.mxf.rationale: MXF (SMPTE 377M) is a documented broadcast/camera-original container read by ffmpeg.
.mxf.migration: Retain the original MXF; keep camera-original media intact.

.ai.rationale: Adobe Illustrator: proprietary, but modern .ai is PDF-compatible and readable.
.ai.migration: Export a PDF or SVG rendition for archival.

.docx.rationale: OOXML is a published standard but complex; LibreOffice reads it with good fidelity.
.docx.migration: Export a PDF for a fixed, archival rendition.

.xlsx.rationale: OOXML spreadsheet: published but complex; LibreOffice/openpyxl read it.
.xlsx.migration: Export CSV (data) and/or PDF (layout) for archival.

.braw.rationale: Blackmagic RAW: a single-vendor camera-original video format with an SDK but no independent reader.
.braw.migration: Retain the original .braw; keep a widely-supported (e.g. ProRes/H.264) delivery rendition alongside it.

.r3d.rationale: RED raw video: proprietary, single-vendor camera original with no independent reader.
.r3d.migration: Retain the original .r3d; keep a delivery rendition (ProRes/MP4) for access.

.lrcat.rationale: Lightroom catalog: a proprietary schema atop SQLite with no full independent reader. It is edit/organizational state, NOT your originals.
.lrcat.migration: Archive your ORIGINAL images and exported renditions; never rely on the catalog as the archive.

.cocatalogdb.rationale: Capture One catalog: proprietary, single-vendor, no independent reader.
.cocatalogdb.migration: Archive the original images and exports; the catalog is not the originals.

.cosessiondb.rationale: Capture One session: proprietary, single-vendor, no independent reader.
.cosessiondb.migration: Archive the original images and exports; the session is not the originals.

.als.rationale: Ableton Live Set: a proprietary gzipped-XML project referencing your audio; no independent editor. It is arrangement/edit state, NOT your stems.
.als.migration: Archive the ORIGINAL audio stems and a rendered mixdown; keep the .als, but never rely on it as the archive.

.flp.rationale: FL Studio project: proprietary, single-vendor, no independent reader. Arrangement/edit state, not the audio.
.flp.migration: Archive the original stems and a rendered mixdown; the .flp is not the audio.

.logicx.rationale: Logic Pro project: proprietary, single-vendor (macOS-only), no independent reader. Arrangement/edit state, not the audio.
.logicx.migration: Archive the original stems and a rendered mixdown; the .logicx is not the audio.

.prproj.rationale: Premiere Pro project: proprietary edit-decision/state referencing your footage; no independent reader. NOT your footage.
.prproj.migration: Archive the ORIGINAL footage and a rendered delivery file; keep the .prproj, but never rely on it as the archive.

.drp.rationale: DaVinci Resolve project: proprietary edit/grade state referencing your footage; no independent reader. NOT your footage.
.drp.migration: Archive the original footage and a rendered delivery; the .drp is not the footage.

.aep.rationale: After Effects project: proprietary composition/edit state referencing your assets; no independent reader. NOT your assets.
.aep.migration: Archive the original source assets and a rendered output; the .aep is not the assets.

.aae.rationale: Apple photo-edit sidecar: Apple-specific non-destructive edit instructions with no general reader.
.aae.migration: Keep the original image; the .aae edits may not port outside Apple Photos.
